Specifically, I'm zeroing in on the goal to develop a complete SPA for "Cristiam Auto Mecânica" using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. This will be the foundational starting point.


**Analyzing the Requirements**

I've been meticulously examining the information provided, particularly the PDF prompt. I'm noting the business details, desired tech stack (React, Tailwind), and design preferences (blue, gray, green, modern). I'm carefully reconciling the "Single File Application" mention with the requirement for a standard React project structure. I'm focusing on the different sections (header, hero, etc.) and features like a sticky header.


**Implementing Features and Data**

I'm now fully immersed in the practical implementation. I'm focusing on incorporating the carousel, lightbox, and the WhatsApp integration. I have the static data sorted; I am structuring the provided JSON objects for company info, services, and testimonials.


**Structuring the Components**

I'm now deep in defining the project's architecture, pinpointing key components like `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and the static data source in `constants.ts`. I'm also planning the types for this data with `types.ts`. I'm focusing on crafting individual sections like the `Header`, `Hero`, `About`, `Services`, `Gallery`, `Testimonials`, and more.


**Defining Interaction and States**

I'm presently focusing on the interactive elements and application states. I'm exploring the icons with 'react-icons', ensuring a mobile-first responsive approach utilizing Tailwind. I'm carefully determining the state management for the mobile menu, the lightbox, and form input. Furthermore, I'm constructing the WhatsApp redirect URL and preparing to implement counters with the use of custom hooks. The Carousel will involve auto-scroll effect with `useEffect`.


**Constructing Visual Elements**

I'm presently building the `Gallery` component, and I'm using placeholder images from `picsum.photos` until I get the client's actual photos. I've also implemented the contact form, which is now ready to open WhatsApp, based on the input field entries. I've set up the `constants.ts` file and copied the JSON exactly, created the interfaces for `Service`, `Testimonial`, and `CompanyInfo` in `types.ts`, and defined a fixed header with z-index. The Hero section now has the correct height on different devices. Finally, the Whatsapp form now utilizes `window.open`. I've set up the images to be from `https://picsum.photos/...`, and imported the required icons from `lucide-react`, and only used tailwind classes.


**Clarifying the Single File Concept**

I'm now reconciling the "Single File Application" prompt with a standard React project. I am striving to make the project robust while adhering to the standard file structure for maintainability. I'm focusing on ensuring the project works seamlessly, even if deployed as a structured React app. I have double-checked the phone number and WhatsApp number, as well as the client's colors.


**Building the Structure**

I'm now focusing on the components' assembly. I'm building `components/Features.tsx`, `components/Gallery.tsx`, `components/Testimonials.tsx`, and `components/Contact.tsx`, which includes the form and map information. The `components/Footer.tsx` is also underway, and I'm finalizing `App.tsx` to integrate everything seamlessly.


**Generating Implementation Details**

I've decided on a custom hook for the intersection observer, opting for a 'Senior' code approach. I've also refined the WhatsApp form functionality. I'm now crafting the WhatsApp redirect URL with the message, name, vehicle, and problem. I'm also ready to build the new files.

verifique se é Single File Application (SFA) pronta para deploy via FTP simples (sem Node.js/Build Step).
Requisitos Técnicos Obrigatórios:
Arquitetura: Todo o código (HTML, CSS, JS, Componentes React) deve residir em um único arquivo index.html.
Stack:
React 18: Importado via ESM (esm.sh) usando <script type="importmap">.
Compilador: Use Babel Standalone (@babel/standalone) para processar JSX no navegador.
Estilização: Use Tailwind CSS via CDN (<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com">). Configure o tema dentro de uma tag <script> no head.
Ícones: Use lucide-react via ESM.
Estrutura:
Mantenha os dados (textos, listas) em constantes separadas no topo do script para fácil edição.
Crie componentes funcionais modulares dentro do mesmo script.
Deploy: O resultado final deve ser apenas um arquivo pronto para ser jogado na pasta httpdocs ou public_html.

**Conceptualizing SFA Architecture**

I'm now drafting the content for `index.html`. This involves incorporating all the merged code. The goal is a truly self-contained file with the final application logic, while fulfilling the single-file requirement. I'm ensuring everything is included within the `<script>` tag.

The `iconName` string from `constants.ts` is essential for the `Services` component to use the correct icons from `lucide-react`. I'm going to maintain the mapping inside the script as `IconMap`. Moreover, I'm working on integrating the external `metadata.json` data into the HTML `<head>` for the title and description, as the goal is a true single-file application.

This should ensure reliable "drop-in FTP" deployment. I'll stick to the user's provided dependencies or use React 18.

I'm now prioritizing the final version dependencies, specifically React and `lucide-react`. I've settled on React 18.2.0 for stability, as it is the "gold standard" for this type of application, and will also preserve `lucide-react` version.
